claims.claim: At 0°C, the capacity retention rate of SIBs is 13.93% higher than that of LIBs at 0.5C discharge rate.
claims.quote: at 0°C, the capacity retention rates further decline to 90.20 % for SIBs and 76.26 % for LIBs

claims.claim: Under maximum overcharge conditions (15%), SIBs experienced thermal runaway reaching a peak temperature of 273.9°C, while LIBs did not exhibit thermal runaway under identical heating conditions.
claims.quote: the data reveal that under identical heating conditions, the SIB experienced thermal runaway, whereas the LIB did not... the peak temperature at T2 reached 273.9°C

claims.claim: After 200 cycles at 3C discharge rate, the capacity retention rate of SIBs is 15.81% lower than that of LIBs.
claims.quote: At discharge rates of 0.5 C, 1 C, 1.5 C, 2 C, and 3 C, the capacity retention rates for SIBs are 97.51 %, 93.45 %, 90.29 %, 85.44 %, and 79.28 %, respectively. In comparison, LIBs exhibit capacity retention rates of 97.63 %, 96.66 %, 96.18 %, 94.53 %, and 95.09 %

claims.claim: At 15% overcharge level, the residual capacity of SIBs was 67.72%, which is 33.35% lower than that of LIBs, while LIBs maintained 99.07% residual capacity.
claims.quote: For SIBs, the residual capacities of the NB and OB at 5 %, 10 %, and 15 % overcharging were 99.70 %, 97.65 %, 92.08 %, and 67.72 %, respectively... the capacity retention rate of SIBs is 33.35 % lower than that of LIBs

claims.claim: Under normal operating conditions at maximum discharge rate (3C), SIBs generate less surface heat than LIBs by 2.683°C, but under maximum overcharging (15%), SIBs produce more surface heat than LIBs by 1.53°C.
claims.quote: Under normal working conditions at the maximum discharge rate (3 C), SIBs generate less surface heat than LIBs, with a difference of 2.683°C. However, under the maximum overcharging level (15 %) in abusive conditions, SIBs produce more surface heat than LIBs, with a difference of 1.53°C

claims.claim: The bottom vent of the 21,700 M50 cylindrical cell with NMC811 cathode undergoes mechanical rupture when internal gas pressure reaches 2 MPa.
claims.quote: Gulsoy et al. [13] experimentally determined that the bottom vent of the 21,700 M50 cylindrical cell with Li(Ni0.8Co0.1Mn0.1)O2 (NMC811) cathode undergoes mechanical rupture when the internal gas pressure reaches 2 MPa.

claims.claim: During thermal runaway of the 21,700 M50 NMC811 cell, gas composition includes hydrogen at 25.3%, carbon dioxide at 38.2%, carbon monoxide at 16.2%, methane at 17.6%, and ethane at 2.7%.
claims.quote: Abbott et al. [14] analyzed the gas composition generated during TR of the same cell, with hydrogen accounting for 25.3%, carbon dioxide at 38.2%, carbon monoxide at 16.2%, methane at 17.6%, and ethane at 2.7%.

claims.claim: The onset temperature (Tonset) at which temperature rise rate increases significantly and consistently corresponds to 105 °C, representing departure from quasi-equilibrium transitioning from stable self-heating (Stage II) to violently accelerated heat release (Stage III).
claims.quote: Tonset is the initial temperature at which the temperature rise rate increases significantly and consistently, corresponding to 105 °C in this study. This temperature point represents the departure of the system from quasi-equilibrium, transitioning from a relatively stable self-heating (Stage II) to violently accelerated heat release (Stage III).

claims.claim: The thermal runaway onset temperature TTR, defined by a temperature rise rate of 1 °C/s, was observed at 143 °C for the 21,700 M50 NMC811 cell under EV-ARC test.
claims.quote: TTR refers to the onset temperature of TR, defined by a temperature rise rate of 1 °C/s, and is observed at 143 °C.

claims.claim: The maximum temperature and maximum thermal rate of the 21,700 M50 LIB cell during the thermal runaway process reached 728 °C and 7688 °C/min, respectively, under EV-ARC test.
claims.quote: The maximum temperature and maximum thermal rate of the LIB cell during the TR process reached 728 °C and 7688 °C/min, respectively.

claims.claim: In NCM811|AG battery with 1M LiPF6 EC/EMC electrolyte, the three characteristic temperatures during ARC test were T1=65°C (onset of self-heating), T2=218°C (thermal runaway trigger, defined when temperature rise rate reached 1°C/s), and T3=750°C (maximum temperature).
claims.quote: In this study, the three characteristic temperatures were located at 65℃ (T1), 218℃ (T2) and 750℃ (T3), respectively.

claims.claim: For the Ca+Ely sample (charged NCM811 cathode mixed with electrolyte), two DSC exothermic peaks appeared at 215°C and 230°C, and the reaction between cathode and electrolyte was identified as the thermal runaway trigger based on the time order of reactions.
claims.quote: As shown in Fig. 1a, for the Ca +Ely sample, two peaks appeared at 215℃ and 230℃. And for the Ca +Ely +An sample, a sharp exothermic peak was also detected at 215℃. Based on the time order of the reactions, the reaction between cathode and electrolyte was identified as the thermal runaway trigger.

claims.claim: For dry charged NCM811 cathode, the oxygen gas release peak initiated at approximately 240°C; however, when electrolyte was added, a sharp oxygen gas peak centered at approximately 230°C was detected, indicating that electrolyte presence shifted and sharpened the oxygen release.
claims.quote: For the dry cathode, the peak initiated at ca. 240oC, which was delayed to the phase transformation. However, the oxygen gas release behavior of the cathode with an electrolyte addition was quite different and a sharp oxygen gas peak centered at ~230oC was detected.

claims.claim: When TPFPB (an anion receptor) was added to the electrolyte in NCM811 full batteries, T2 decreased to 198°C (20°C lower than for NCM811 without TPFPB), indicating that TPFPB addition made the battery more susceptible to thermal runaway onset, attributed to TPFPB reducing oxygen species to oxygen gas at lower temperature.
claims.quote: The key temperature, T2, was 198 oC for B-NCM811, 20 oC lower than for NCM811, indicating that the battery was more likely to thermal runaway after TPFPB added. By connecting the abovementioned profiles of oxygen release for the charged NCM811 cathodes without and in the presence of TPFPB (Fig. 3e), the lower T2 could be mainly attributed to the reducibility of TPFPB, which makes oxygen gas release at lower temperature.

claims.claim: In the presence of electrolyte, the lattice c of charged NCM811 cathode exhibits moderate evolution and strain is released spontaneously by cation migration, phase transformation and oxygen release, whereas the dry cathode can endure much greater lattice strain, meaning electrolyte accelerates phase transition and oxygen release.
claims.quote: the dry cathode can withhold more c lattice expansion than it in the presence of electrolyte and TPFPB, thereby the oxygen release becomes more difficult... the strain in the presence of electrolyte is released spontaneously by cation migration, phase transformation and oxygen release. Therefore, the results show that electrolyte has a negative impact on the phase structure stability of cathode, which could accelerate phase transition and oxygen release.
